accept: ResolveHook recognizes the bare op-type name (create/close/claim) matching the docs and the existing .act/hooks/close file. Existing per-repo hooks at .act/hooks/<op> fire end-to-end.
TestResolveHookMapsOpType (and any sibling tests) updated to reflect the new naming; coverage retained.
Running act close in a repo with gofmt drift fails with the hook's [hook] FAIL output and rolls back the close.
Doc-drift prevention: a regression test asserts ResolveHook resolves '.act/hooks/close' when an executable file named 'close' is present (catches the resolver/docs mismatch should it ever return).
payload.description: internal/hooks/hooks.go:108 ResolveHook maps op_type -> hook filename via the recognized map ({create: post-create, close: post-close, claim: post-claim}). But CLAUDE.md, the act-skill SKILL.md, and the .act/hooks/close file in this repo all use the BARE op-type name (close, not post-close). ResolveHook therefore returns ("", false) on every close → the hook silently doesn't fire → gofmt/vet/test gates that the hook is supposed to enforce locally don't actually run before close commits → CI catches drift on push that should have been caught locally. Concrete evidence: .act/hooks/close has been a no-op since act-8534 (the file was created with the bare name); the recent close.go + config.go gofmt drift reached main because no close went through the gate. Confirmed by reproducing: with the hook executable present and gofmt -l . non-empty, act close succeeds with exit 0 and no [hook] output.
payload.title: act-repo hooks never fire: resolver expects post-<op>, file is .act/hooks/close
